sources.reviewedCode:
# Time:  O(n!)
# Space: O(n)

# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Every newly reached node may add more nodes to the frontier, so keep exploring until the frontier is empty.
class Solution(object):
    def canVisitAllRooms(self, rooms):
        """
        :type rooms: List[List[int]]
        :rtype: bool
        """
        # [Reader note] State setup
        # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Visited tracking, graph reachability, and seeing traversal as unlocking more future work.
        lookup = set([0])
        stack = [0]
        # [Reader note] Main pass
        # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
        while stack:
            node = stack.pop()
            for nei in rooms[node]:
                # [Reader note] Decision point
                # Pay attention here: The concept is gentle; the only real mistake is forgetting that keys can reveal more keys recursively.
                if nei not in lookup:
                    lookup.add(nei)
                    if len(lookup) == len(rooms):
                        return True
                    stack.append(nei)
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return len(lookup) == len(rooms)
